_comment: Manifest read by installed copies of Whisper Dictate. Updated by release-public.ps1 as part of the dual-publish step. The 'version' field follows SemVer; the 'installer_url' must point at a downloadable .exe asset on a public release of this repo.
release_notes_md:
### Added

- **"What's new" tab in Settings.** Renders `WHATS_NEW.md` (user-facing condensed release notes, one sentence per item) as scrollable formatted text. Lets users browse what shipped in every version, not just the latest. Two-file architecture: `CHANGELOG.md` stays detailed and developer-facing (this file, shown on GitHub); `WHATS_NEW.md` is the condensed companion, ships with the installer, and is rendered in the tab via the same markdown helper the post-update "What's new" modal uses, so formatting is consistent across both surfaces. Maintenance note: every CHANGELOG bump needs a matching condensed entry in WHATS_NEW.
- **Recording-elapsed counter on the overlay.** The `â—Ź Recording` overlay now shows a live elapsed-seconds counter (`â—Ź Recording (12s)`, `â—Ź Recording (1m 40s)`) so the user knows at a glance how long they've been dictating â€” useful both for pacing long messages and for noticing when they've been silently waiting for something (e.g. a thought that hasn't come) instead of recording. Pause/resume preserves the counter: pausing freezes the displayed value (engine doesn't tick recorded-seconds while paused), resume continues counting from there rather than restarting at zero. Implementation mirrors the existing transcribing countdown â€” `dictate.py` includes `recorded_seconds` in every recording-state heartbeat (`0.0` on idleâ†’recording, accumulated duration on pausedâ†’recording); `main_app.py` anchors against that value plus the heartbeat's `ts` and extrapolates forward by wall-clock delta on every poll tick.
- **"Copy last transcription" recovery action.** Lives both as a tray menu item and as a button on the main window. Reads `last_transcription.txt` (newly written by `dictate.py` before every paste delivery) and places its contents on the clipboard so the user can paste it manually wherever they actually wanted. Solves the false-negative case where paste landed in the wrong place but the focus-lost detector didn't fire â€” the v0.4.0 `PasteWarningToast` covers true-positive focus drift; this covers everything else. A short confirmation toast (`âś“ Copied: <preview>â€¦`) appears on success, and `No transcription yet` if the user clicks before any dictation has happened in the current install.

### Fixed

- **Update progress was invisible in tray-only mode** â€” clicking "Update now" in v0.4.0 opened a Tk progress dialog `transient` to the main window and centered over it. The main window is normally hidden in tray-only operation, so the dialog was positioned at coordinates derived from a hidden-window's geometry (often off-screen) and missing `-topmost`, leaving the user staring at a (frozen-looking) main window with the actual download running invisibly in the background. Replaced with `UpdateProgressToast` â€” same bottom-right slot as the `UpdateToast` the user just clicked, slate-blue chrome matching it for visual continuity, `overrideredirect` + Win32-level topmost so it can't be hidden behind other apps. Cancel as a clickable `tk.Label` (not `tk.Button`) â€” same Win32 lesson as `UpdateToast` and `PasteWarningToast`. The main window is also now defensively `withdraw()`'d on update-start and restored on cancel/error.
- **Patch updates reinstalled all ~80 Python dependencies, taking 5+ minutes for an app-code-only change.** The Inno Setup script wiped `runtime\Lib\site-packages` on every install and re-ran `pip install -r requirements.txt`, downloading or extracting torch + openvino + transformers et al. unconditionally â€” even when `requirements.txt` hadn't changed. New hash-gate: `build.ps1` computes `SHA256(requirements.txt + python.exe)` and patches it into `dictate.iss` as `#define RequirementsHash`. At install time, `NeedsPipInstall` (Pascal) compares this against `{app}\app\.requirements.sha256` (marker written by the previous successful install) and, on match, skips both the site-packages wipe AND the pip step entirely. Patch updates with unchanged deps now complete in seconds. Mismatch (or fresh install, or `/FORCEPIP=1` on the installer command line) falls back to the legacy wipe + full reinstall path. Marker is written in `CurStepChanged(ssPostInstall)` so a failed install never leaves a stale-but-correct-looking gate. Reasoning and pitfall mitigations cross-checked against documented Inno Setup primitives (`GetSHA256OfFile`, `Check:`, `LoadStringFromFile`, `SaveStringToFile`) and against industry patterns (Squirrel.Windows, MSIX block-map, JetBrains binary patches) â€” see MAINTAINERS.md for the threat model and the `/FORCEPIP=1` escape hatch.
